verdict: While the chart accurately syncs to the track's dreamy bridge, the drop suffers from extreme repetition and several highly awkward transitions that spike the difficulty past a Medium level.

issues.problem: The chorus repeatedly loops the exact same 'LR Jump -> U -> D -> L' 8th-note pattern. This is highly monotonous, tiring, and fails to capture the glitchy, dynamic details of the song's production.
Awkward transition from a single tap on L (68.673) directly into a LD jump (68.946), forcing an uncomfortable 'one-foot' jump since the L panel is shared.
Similarly to 68.946, transitioning from a tap on L (86.135) directly into a LD jump (86.408) disrupts natural foot flow.
Dense 16th-note streams containing rapid direction changes (like D->L->U->D and R->L->D->U) introduce crossovers and double-steps that are too physically complex for a standard Medium chart.
A rapid 16th-note transition from a single D tap (75.358) to a LR jump (75.494) creates a sudden, awkward physical spike.
